Title: CYP76BK1 orthologs catalyze furan and lactone ring formation in clerodane diterpenoids across the mint family

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: bioactive diterpenoids with insect-antifeedant and antimicrobial activities
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Catalyzes oxidative cyclization of clerodane substrates to form furan and lactone ring-containing clerodane diterpenoid intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; GC-MS; NMR structural elucidation; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ArCYP76BK1 converted kolavenol/isokolavenol-derived clerodane backbones into furan products and minor lactone products. 2. It was one of the first two orthologs identified to catalyze furanoclerodane formation in divergent Lamiaceae species. 3. It also showed some activity toward peregrinol-derived labdane substrate, supporting substrate promiscuity.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: oxidative tailoring/cyclization branch forming furan and lactone rings
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate intermediates toward target furanoclerodanes
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Performs successive oxidations on clerodane/labdane side chains, enabling spontaneous cyclization to furan and lactone ring systems
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key enzyme in furanoclerodane biosynthesis across Lamiaceae
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: leaf cDNA cloned; native leaf expression data available
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient Agrobacterium-mediated expression in N. benthamiana leaves under controlled growth conditions
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptomics for candidate identification in A. reptans; T. chamaedrys RNA-seq BioProject PRJNA1124528 (study-wide)
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: A. reptans appears to have only an iso-KPP synthase, suggesting substrate availability may shape in planta product outcome more than CYP76BK1 selectivity.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful enzyme for engineering sustainable production of bioactive furanoclerodanes and lactone derivatives in heterologous platforms.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: C. americana leaf extracts; transient N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: bioactive diterpenoids with insect-antifeedant and antimicrobial activities
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Catalyzes oxidative cyclization to generate furanoclerodane intermediates and low levels of lactone derivatives
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; GC-MS; NMR; analysis of C. americana leaf extracts
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CamCYP76BK1 was identified as a core P450 producing furanoclerodane intermediates from clerodane substrates. 2. Product 2 and lactones 4 and 6 were detected in C. americana leaf extracts, supporting in planta relevance. 3. CamCYP76BK1 showed relatively low activity for lactone formation compared with several orthologs.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: oxidative tailoring/cyclization branch forming furan and lactone rings
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate intermediates toward target furanoclerodanes
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes clerodane side chains to enable furan ring formation and additional oxidation-driven lactone formation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: selected using tissue-specific expression correlated with CamTPS2
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient Agrobacterium-mediated expression in N. benthamiana leaves; native metabolite detection in leaves
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genomic and tissue-specific expression data used for candidate selection in C. americana
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Neither CamCYP76BK1 nor VacCYP76BK1 appeared capable of converting isokolavenol to detectable furan or lactone products in comparative assays.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for metabolic engineering of furanoclerodane scaffolds, especially when combined with upstream clerodane synthases and additional oxidases to improve lactone output.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Shows moderate activity with kolavenol and low activity generating furan/lactone products on clerodane and labdane substrates; previously known for C16 hydroxylation of peregrinol
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; GC-MS
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VacCYP76BK1 is a founding CYP76BK1 ortholog with related oxidative cyclization activity. 2. In this study it generated detectable furan/lactone derivatives from clerodane/labdane substrates, though at low activity. 3. It broadens the functional scope beyond previously reported C16 hydroxylation of peregrinol.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Carries out side-chain oxidations including C16 hydroxylation and additional oxidations leading to furan/lactone ring formation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key oxidative tailoring enzyme, not established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient Agrobacterium-mediated expression in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Could not convert isokolavenol to detectable furan or lactone products under tested conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Supports use of CYP76BK1 clade enzymes as toolbox components for custom oxidation chemistry in diterpenoid engineering.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: furan and lactone clerodane diterpenoid intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Catalyzes formation of furan and lactone products from clerodane substrates, with preference toward C16 lactone formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; GC-MS
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CpCYP76BK1 belongs to the conserved CYP76BK1 clade across Lamiaceae. 2. It generated the same major furan/lactone product classes as other orthologs. 3. Its product profile favored the C16 lactone relative to the C15 lactone.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate intermediates toward target furanoclerodanes
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: furan and C16/C15 lactone clerodane derivatives
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidative tailoring of clerodane side chains leading to spontaneous furan or lactone cyclization
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key oxidative tailoring enzyme in ortholog set
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient Agrobacterium-mediated expression in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome mining from Mint Genome Project
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Species leaf extract did not show exact CYP76BK1 products under tested conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential enzyme choice when aiming to bias diterpenoid engineering toward C16 lactone-containing scaffolds.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: furan and lactone clerodane diterpenoid intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Catalyzes formation of furan and lactone products, with preference toward C15 lactone accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; GC-MS
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PbCYP76BK1 is a CYP76BK1 ortholog from a species without reported diterpenoids. 2. It nonetheless catalyzed furan/lactone formation on clerodane substrates in heterologous assays. 3. Its product profile favored C15 lactone over C16 lactone.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate intermediates toward target furanoclerodanes
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes clerodane side chains to generate furan and especially C15 lactone products
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key oxidative tailoring enzyme in ortholog set
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient Agrobacterium-mediated expression in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome mining from Mint Genome Project
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Presence of CYP76BK1 suggests this species may harbor unreported furanoclerodane chemistry.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful ortholog for engineering pathways enriched in C15 lactone clerodane products.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: furan and lactone clerodane/labdane diterpenoid intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Efficiently catalyzes conversion to furan products and favors C16 lactone formation; also active on peregrinol-derived substrate
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; GC-MS; NMR-supported product scale-up for one lactone derivative
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HsCYP76BK1 gave the best conversion of kolavenol to the furan product 1 among tested orthologs. 2. It preferentially formed the C16 lactone branch. 3. It also produced putative peregrinol-derived lactone/furan products, indicating substrate promiscuity.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: furan and C16 lactone formation branch
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate intermediates toward target furanoclerodanes/furanolabdanes
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: furan product 1; C16 lactone product 3; putative labdane furan/lactones
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Performs multi-step oxidations that strongly support furan formation and C16 lactonization on diterpene side chains
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key oxidative tailoring enzyme in ortholog set
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient Agrobacterium-mediated expression in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome mining from Mint Genome Project
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Species has no reported diterpenoids in literature, so enzyme discovery suggests unexplored metabolic potential.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate ortholog for heterologous production of furan and C16 lactone diterpenoid scaffolds.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: furan and lactone clerodane diterpenoid intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Catalyzes furan and lactone formation with strongest accumulation of the putative C15 lactone product 6
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SbCYP76BK1 generated the common CYP76BK1 product spectrum. 2. It afforded the highest yield of the putative C15 lactone product 6. 3. Genomic analysis supports CYP76BK1 as a syntenic ortholog in Scutellarioideae.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: furan and C15 lactone formation branch
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate intermediates toward target furanoclerodanes
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: furan derivatives; putative C15 lactone product 6
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Performs iterative oxidation reactions leading to furan and especially C15 lactone cyclization products
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key oxidative tailoring enzyme in ortholog set
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: leaf cDNA cloned / ortholog identified in transcriptomic and genomic data
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient Agrobacterium-mediated expression in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper text also mentions SbarbCYP76BK1 in scale-up procedures, indicating closely related Scutellaria ortholog usage for purification of one product.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising ortholog for engineering C15 lactone-enriched furanoclerodane pathways.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: furan and lactone clerodane/labdane diterpenoid intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; GC-MS; NMR-supported product scale-up
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CbCYP76BK1 was most active against isokolavenol and gave the best yield of compound 4. 2. Its product profile preferentially accumulated the C16 lactone. 3. It also showed detectable activity on peregrinol-derived substrate.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: furan and C16 lactone formation branch
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate intermediates toward target furanoclerodanes/furanolabdanes
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: compound 4; C16 lactone products; putative labdane products
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidatively tailors diterpene side chains to favor furan and C16 lactone cyclization, especially from isokolavenol-derived substrate
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key oxidative tailoring enzyme in ortholog set
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient Agrobacterium-mediated expression in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome mining from Mint Genome Project
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used for preparative production of CamTPS2-derived furan and lactone derivatives for NMR analysis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong ortholog for pathway engineering when high isokolavenol conversion or C16 lactone output is desired.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: furan and lactone clerodane diterpenoid intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Ortholog identified in phylogeny as member of CYP76BK1 clade; evaluated functionally with clerodane substrates to produce characteristic furan/lactone products
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; GC-MS
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TcaCYP76BK1 was identified as a CYP76BK1 ortholog in the mint family survey. 2. Functional assays showed the shared CYP76BK1 activity pattern on clerodane substrates. 3. It supports wide conservation of the pathway across subfamilies.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate intermediates toward target furanoclerodanes
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidative cyclization of clerodane side chains to produce furan/lactone intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key oxidative tailoring enzyme in ortholog set
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient Agrobacterium-mediated expression in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome mining from Mint Genome Project
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: T. chamaedrys transcriptome revealed a very close homolog (TchCYP76BK1, 94.7% identical to TcaCYP76BK1).
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Expands available ortholog set for engineering furanoclerodane biosynthesis.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: furan and lactone clerodane diterpenoid intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Catalyzes characteristic CYP76BK1 furan/lactone formation, with product bias toward C15 lactone accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcriptome assembly; transient co-expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; GC-MS
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TchCYP76BK1 was identified from a newly generated T. chamaedrys transcriptome. 2. Functional assays showed shared CYP76BK1 activity on clerodane substrates. 3. Its product profile favored the C15 lactone relative to the C16 lactone.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: furan and C15 lactone formation branch
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate intermediates toward target furanoclerodanes
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Performs iterative oxidation of clerodane side chains enabling furan and preferential C15 lactone cyclization
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key oxidative tailoring enzyme in ortholog set
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient Agrobacterium-mediated expression in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq transcriptome assembly; raw reads in BioProject PRJNA1124528
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful ortholog for pathway engineering and for exploring sequence determinants of C15 lactone bias.
